channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Rekkles Isn’t Retiring Despite Los Ratones and Witchcraft Ending
item.content:encoded:
Iconic League of Legends pro Martin ‘Rekkles’ Larsson has confirmed he is not planning on retiring from his LoL esports career just yet.

The 29-year-old is a four-time LEC champion and has stated that he plans to fight for a spot on a Tier 1 team next year, whether that’s as an ADC, support, or coach.

Following the end of Marc ‘Caedrel’ Lamont’s Los Ratones project, the roster continued as ‘Witchcraft’ in EMEA Masters Winter with the addition of Gabriël ‘Bwipo’ Rau.

However, Witchcraft was shockingly eliminated early from EMEA Masters Winter by G2 Nord, the academy of LEC champions G2 Esports, and now the Swede is without a team.

His LEC Versus campaign with Los Ratones was Rekkles’ first time competing in the EMEA league since 2023 and proved that he is still capable of competing at an elite level.

What Is Next For Rekkles?

Speaking on his Twitch stream, Rekkles revealed his plans for the remainder of the year.

The former Fnatic and G2 pro says his main focus is on the November transfer window in the offseason and he will use the rest of 2026 to prove that he deserves a spot on a team next year.

Rekkles plans on co-streaming international events like LoL First Stand and MSI with a “big picture League of Legends focus” to practice and showcase his coaching abilities.

Then, Rekkles will be bootcamping in South Korea for over a month, between May 4 and June 15, where he will be practising as both an ADC and support.

After a successful 11 years as an ADC, Rekkles roleswapped to the less-mechanically intense support role for the 2024 season with T1 Academy. Now, Rekkles is seemingly considering returning to his original role.

Rekkles says after spending the year grinding, he will go on vacation on August 3 and, from there, will reassess exactly what he wants heading into the offseason.

Does Rekkles still have what it takes to compete in the LEC? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And look forward to the next LoL esports season as LoL Worlds 2027 dates have been revealed.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Pokémon GO Data Will Help Autonomous Robots Make Deliveries
item.content:encoded:
A new report reveals that data collected from Pokémon GO players will be used to help robots make precise deliveries.

Pokémon GO is an augmented reality game developed and released by Niantic in 2016, and it was an undeniable success. It brought in millions of players across the globe, all scouring through the streets in the hope of catching ’em all. This process has now enabled the company to collect data that has improved its GPS tech tremendously.

Niantic Spatial Has Been Building a Visual Positioning System from Pokémon GO Players’ Data

According to a report by MIT Technology Review (via GamesRadar+), Niantic Spatial has been utilizing data from Pokémon GO to enhance its visual positioning system for the past few years. The new model has been trained on over a million locations worldwide and can accurately pinpoint a user’s location. The level of precision is exceptionally high; Niantic Spatial CEO John Hanke stated that it could achieve accuracy within several centimeters and can also determine the direction in which a user is looking.

The company’s short-term goal is to use this data to help autonomous robots from Coco Robotics deliver with higher accuracy across Europe and the United States. The new model has been trained on 30 billion images that were captured by Pokémon Go players. The CTO, Brian McClendon, has also spoken about long-term plans, stating, “We’re not there yet, but we want to be there. I’m very focused on trying to re-create the real world.”

In other news, here’s a guide on the Pokémon GO 30th anniversary Pressure Rising Special Research, and all the rewards. Additionally, Pokémon Pokopia sold over 2.2 million copies within the first four days. What are your thoughts on Pokémon GO data being used to help robots make deliveries? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Saudi Arabia’s Electronic Gaming Development Company acquiring a 5% stake in Capcom. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Life Is Strange: Reunion Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times
item.content:encoded:
The Life Is Strange series dates all the way back to 2015, and now, over 10 years later, Max and Chloe’s story appears to be coming to an end with Life Is Strange: Reunion.

It’s been a journey full of ups and downs, but it’s undeniable that countless fans have resonated with the ever-evolving narrative. If you were there since the start, or a new fan looking for a heart-wrenching story, then make sure you don’t miss Life Is Strange: Reunion’s release date.

When Is Life Is Strange: Reunion Coming Out? Release Date, Explained

There’s not long left, as Life Is Strange: Reunion releases as a full game on March 26, 2026.

Unlike previous entries in the series, Life Is Strange: Reunion will not be episodically released, meaning you can experience the full adventure all at once.

  • More Insider Gaming: Pokémon TCG Chaos Rising: Release Date & Card List

It’s a bold choice, but undoubtedly a welcome one for die-hard fans who are desperate to know how the narrative unfolds. Depending on who you are, there’s often nothing worse than being left on a cliffhanger and waiting months to see it resolved, but thankfully, that won’t be the case here.

Life Is Strange: Reunion Release Times

According to SteamDB, Life Is Strange: Reunion will go live at 9 AM PT on the 26 March, 2026.

  • More Insider Gaming: Battlefield 6 Season 2 Nightfall Release Date

It’s great news for those on the West Coast, as you can book the day off and sink your teeth into what’s bound to be a gripping narrative full of choices to make. For the rest of you, though, here’s when Life Is Strange: Reunion will release across a variety of other time zones:

Are you excited to play Life Is Strange: Reunion? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more important release dates, check out when the Zenless Zone Zero Update 2.7 and Honkai Star Rail Update 4.1 come out.

item.title: Zenless Zone Zero Update 2.7 Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times
item.content:encoded:
If you’re a fan of popular free-to-play gacha games, then we probably don’t need to remind you that Zenless Zone Zero Update 2.7 is coming. The new update is a big addition for 2026, and this hub has the latest on its release date and start times.

Does anyone do F2P gacha games better than miHoYo? Genshin Impact could have been a one-off, but the developer fired off Honkai Star Rail and Zenless Zone Zero in quick succession to keep the momentum going. The latter is what we’re focusing on, in particular, the Zenless Zone Zero Update 2.7.

When Is Zenless Zone Zero Update 2.7 Coming Out? Release Date, Explained

You download and install the Zenless Zone Zero Update 2.7 from March 24.

HoYoverse has confirmed this as the release date, and ‘Champions Never Fall to the Past,’ will include the following: The Hollow Champion competition, new allies in Nangong Yu and Cissia joining the fray, themed activities at the Simmer Hotpot Restaurant, and Simulated Battle Trial tweaks and additions.

  • More Insider Gaming: Honkai Star Rail 4.1 Update Release Date & Start Times

There’s a lot to look forward to, and if version 2.7 is derailed, Insider Gaming will let you know about it.

Zenless Zone Zero 2.7 Update Release Times

Based on previous Zenless Zone Zero updates, you should expect 2.7 to go live from 7 PM PT on March 24.

  • More Insider Gaming: Pokémon TCG Chaos Rising: Release Date & Card List

Whether you’re based elsewhere in America, you live in Europe, or perhaps you’re based slightly further south in Australasia, the table below covers many of the biggest time zones in the world.

Check out when the 2.7 Update drops.

Are you looking forward to being in the zone for the new Zenless Zone Zero content? If you want to talk about the game or anything else in general with the IG community, check out the free-to-join Insider Gaming Discord.

For more news, read about Warner Bros. Montréal being hit by layoffs, and Resident Evil Requiem selling 6 million copies to become the fastest-selling in the series.
